compiled_state.title: Add JSONL schema comment block to apply-attribution-trailers.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: This task is folded into T3a-impl: the schema comment block should be written as part of creating the script skeleton in T3a-impl. If T3a-impl was executed without the schema comment, add a comment block at the top of plugins/dso/scripts/apply-attribution-trailers.sh documenting: JSONL format (one JSON object per line), agent entry format, skill entry format, multi-value trailers (DSO-Agent/DSO-Skill/DSO-Model), scalar trailers (DSO-Task/Story/Epic/Jira-Ticket/DSO-Review-Score), and truncation contract (cleared to 0 bytes after each successful git commit exit 0). TDD exemption: Unit exemption criteria 1 (no conditional logic) and 3 (static assets -- comment block with no executable paths). AC: schema comment present at top of script; validate.sh --ci passes.

comments.body:
Gap Analysis AC amendment: pre-commit stage cannot access COMMIT_MSG_FILE — must use prepare-commit-msg or commit-msg stage.

Git pre-commit hooks run BEFORE the commit message file is created — they do not receive COMMIT_MSG_FILE as an argument. Only prepare-commit-msg and commit-msg stage hooks receive the commit message file path as $1.

Amended wiring approach: Register apply-attribution-trailers.sh as a prepare-commit-msg hook (or commit-msg hook), NOT a pre-commit hook. The prepare-commit-msg stage is preferred (runs before user editor, before commit-msg ticket gate).

For --truncate wiring: There are no post-commit hooks in the current plugin infrastructure. Options:
  (a) Add a post-commit hook stage, or
  (b) Wire --truncate call into COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md instructions from T8 as an orchestrator-level step (not a git hook)

The integration test must use a real git repo and verify trailers appear in git log via a prepare-commit-msg or commit-msg hook, not a pre-commit hook.
comments.author: Test
